title: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba
taxonomy: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba belongs to the family Malvaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it grows in dry areas. The species is classified within the genus Sphaeralcea, which consists of several species of desert marigolds. Sphaeralcea obtusiloba is a perennial herb with yellow flowers, typically found in sandy or rocky soils. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba, also known as the desert marigold or orange globe marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. While there is limited information on its medicinal uses, some sources suggest that it may have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal purposes. Sphaeralcea obtusiloba is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

properties: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba, also known as the blunt yellow marigold, is a plant with potential therapeutic properties. Its roots and flowers are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and infections. constituents: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba, also known as the blunt yellow marigold, contains a variety of active constituents. The plant has been found to contain flavonoids, particularly flavanols and flavonol glycosides. Additionally, Sphaeralcea obtusiloba contains phenolic acids and saponins, which are known for their potential medicinal properties. parts: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba, also known as the blunt globemallow, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine. The roots and leaves of the plant are considered to be the medicinal parts, as they contain various bioactive compounds. preparations: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba, also known as desert marigold, has been used in herbal preparations for its potential medicinal properties. The roots and flowers of the plant are traditionally used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures. These preparations are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. intro: Sphaeralcea obtusiloba, also known as the obtuse globe mallow, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeralcea obtusiloba include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin infections, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
